item.title: Phase equilibrium engineering in biorefinery reactive systems: chemical equilibrium prediction in acetylation of n‐alkanols
item.description: In this work we perform simultaneous phase and chemical equilibrium calculation using the Group Contribution with Association equation of state (GCA-EOS), aiming to assess reactive systems in the framework of biorefinery development. The lack of ...

item.description: Spectroscopic investigation of tellurium-centered hydrogen bonds is relatively scarce. Here, 1:1 complex of dimethyl ditelluride (Me2Te2) and H2S was investigated using matrix isolation infrared spectroscopy and quantum chemical calculations. Only S-H···...

item.title: Engineering Cooperative Multi-Domain DNA Probes for Programmable Nucleic Acid Detection
item.description: Nucleic acids are powerful recognition elements because base pairing enables straightforward probe design, yet selective target recognition under physiological conditions remains challenging when specificity cannot rely on melting-based discrimination. ...

item.title: Fatty Acid Composition in Ocean-Derived Triglyceride Oils and its Influence on Rheology and Chemical Conversion
item.description: Valorization of aquaculture waste into biofuels and biomaterials is crucial to advancing a low-carbon bioeconomy. However, the inherent variability in the fatty acid composition introduces uncertainties in conversion efficiencies during ...

item.title: On Contemporary Tafel Analysis
item.description: Tafel slope analysis has been the cornerstone of mechanistic interpretation in multi-step electrochemical reactions for nearly a century, underpinning our understanding of reactions from hydrogen evolution to carbon dioxide reduction. Here we demonstrate ...

item.title: Cross-species principles of silk protein assembly from metastable feedstocks to hierarchical materials
item.description: Silk is one of nature’s most versatile structural biomaterials, produced independently by a vast diversity of species across insects, spiders, marine invertebrates, crustaceans, and even select vertebrates. Despite their evolutionary distance and ...

item.title: Symmetry Numbers: A Flexible Approach for Molecules and Transition States
item.description: Symmetry numbers are essential corrections in thermochemistry and chemical kinetics, but their reliable assignment remains difficult in automated workflows, especially for stereochemical, fluxional, cyclic, and transition-state structures. We present a ...

item.title: Photoredox/Cobalt Dual Catalysis Enables C(sp3)C(sp2)/C(sp) Bond Construction via a Biomimetic Approach
item.description: We developed a photoredox–cobalt synergistic biomimetic reaction, constructing an unsaturated molecular library. Remarkably, we demonstrate the synthesis of a series of polysubstituted cinnamaldehydes. Furthermore, reported an unprecedented property of α‑...

item.title: Structural flexibility of styrylpyrene chromophore in reproducing solvent-induced spectral shifts
item.description: Solvatochromic shifts in UV-vis absorption of photoreactive chromophores and microenvironment effects in their light-induced [2+2] cycloaddition reactions are presently not fully understood. Spectral shifts observed experimentally could not be yet ...

item.title: Application of photocatalytic degradation of Rhodamine B (A Review) (2021-2025)
item.description: One particularly harmful dye known as Rhodamine B, or RhB, is widely utilized, yet it can linger in aquatic environments for extended periods and poses risks. Traditional methods for purifying water, such as employing chemicals or allowing tiny organisms ...

item.description: High-throughput computational screening of metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) for gas storage and separation applications routinely begins from structural repositories containing tens of thousands of candidates, only a small fraction of which are ...

item.title: Sensitive Derivatization-Free HPLC-MS Method For Quantification of Cyclohexylamine as a Process-Related Impurity in Rosuvastatin calcium salt
item.description: Cyclohexylamine is an alicyclic amine that may be introduced as a process-related impurity during the manufacture of rosuvastatin calcium. Due to its potential toxicological relevance, sensitive and selective analytical methods are required to monitor ...

item.description: Liquid-phase adsorption (LPA) measurements are less frequently applied for the characterisation of porous materials than gas adsorption, although, for a variety of applications, such as (water) purification or heterogeneous catalysis, the liquid phase ...

item.description: Directed evolution is a powerful strategy for enzyme engineering, and has successfully enabled unique stereoselective biosynthesis of high-value organic molecules. Enantiomeric excess (ee) remains the gold standard to assess the enzyme performance, but ...

item.title: Quantum defect density controls carbon nanotube sensor sensitivity via photophysical cooperativity

item.title: Nile Red–phosphate binding macrocycle conjugate for phosphatidylserine sensing
item.description: Detection of phosphatidylserine (PS) in biomembranes is important because externalized PS serves as a functional signal for key biological processes. Towards developing new probes for PS, the phosphate-binding macrocycle was conjugated with polarity-...

item.title: Selenenic vs. Sulfenic Acids: Enthalpies of Formation, Bond Dissociation Energies, and Thermochemical Properties from Composite Methods
item.description: Selenenic acids (RSeOH) are reactive intermediates of atmospheric, biological, and environmental importance, but their thermochemistry has been experimentally inaccessible and computationally under-characterized. We report the first systematic ...

item.title: Cyclization-Mediated Nonradiative Decay in Carbo[n]helicenes (n = 5–12): Reaction Path Analysis of Molecular Size Effects
item.description: Carbo[n]helicenes are helically fused polycyclic hydrocarbons that exhibit low fluorescence quantum yields and predominantly undergo triplet-mediated relaxation. However, their nonradiative decay paths remain unclear. To elucidate the relaxation process, ...

item.title: AI-driven design of a cost-effective synthetic route to malaria drug Ganaplacide
item.description: Malaria is a deadly infectious disease that disproportionately affects third-world countries. Ganaplacide is a drug candidate that is highly effective against malaria, but no affordable way to produce it exists yet. Here we show how we used a combination ...

item.title: Active site density distribution controls the reaction efficiency of methanol conversion
item.description: The transient evolution of fresh catalysts to working catalysts involves an induction period, transition region, and steady-state. There is, however, limited information on how to increase the reaction efficiency. Reaction-diffusion-dynamic coupling is ...

item.title: Deciphering the Chiral Inducer in Micelle-Templated Growth of Chiral Gold Nanorods

item.description: (Dated: July 14, 2026) Simulating the dynamical trajectories of complex metalloenzymes, such as the nitrogenase FeMo-cofactor, remains classically intractable due to real-time entanglement growth and non-adiabatic surface crossings. Here, we introduce the ...

item.title: Reaction-Depth Learning Enables Apparent Yield Extrapolation in Catalyst Informatics
item.description: Extrapolative prediction of high-yield catalyst performance remains challenging because conventional direct-yield machine-learning models do not explicitly represent the target, side, and competing reactions that determine the final yield. Here, we ...

item.title: A Geometric Interpretation of Slater’s Shielding Constants: Radial Overlap as P (rinner < router)
item.description: In 1930, J. C. Slater introduced a set of empirical shielding constants—0.35, 0.85, and 1.00—that remain widely used for estimating effective nuclear charge. Despite ninety-five years of use, a geometric interpretation of these specific values has ...

item.title: NJmatWeb: An AI-Driven Web Platform Automating Machine Learning and Genetic Algorithm for Materials Science
item.description: Data-driven methodologies are now widely employed for predicting materials properties; however, most existing tools fail to integrate automated feature selection, interpretable modeling, and an accessible user interface into a unified pipeline. To bridge ...

item.title: Cholesterol-Dependent Membrane Fluidity in Sindbis Virus Governs Viral Particle Stability and Infectivity

item.title: Electrosynthesis of free-standing asymmetric chitosan films at polarised soft interfaces

item.description: Phenol aggregate populations in carbon tetrachloride are governed by an enthalpyentropy balance that varies with concentration and temperature. This redistribution is reflected in the infrared O H stretching band, whose position and shape provide a ...

item.title: Ball-Milling-Enabled Pd-Catalyzed One-Pot Cross-Coupling of Aryl Sulfonium Salts with In Situ Generated Reformatsky Enolates
item.description: Mechanochemistry enables an efficient Pd-catalyzed cross-coupling of aryl thianthrenium salts with in situ generated Reformatsky-type enolates. Ball milling plays a dual role by promoting both the activation of zinc for the formation of Reformatsky-type ...

item.title: Phase-Aware Adaptation for Functional Group Recognition fromCondensed-Phase Infrared Spectra
item.description: Infrared (IR) spectroscopy is widely used for functional group identification, but machine-learning models often struggle when spectra are transferred across physical phases. Here we establish a phase-separated 16-label benchmark for functional group ...

item.description: Excited-state intramolecular proton transfer is a prototypical ultrafast process that forms the foundation for many chemical and biological phenomena and is highly sensitive to changes in the underlying potential energy landscape. Here, we develop neural ...

item.title: Breaking Enantiopurity Paradigm: A Chiral Catalyst That Requires Enantioimpurity for Enantioselectivity

item.title: Linking wind-power fluctuation to degradation of PEM water electrolyzer
item.description: Proton exchange membrane (PEM) water electrolysis is highly attractive for renewable-powered hydrogen production, yet its degradation under realistic wind-power fluctuations remains insufficiently understood. In this study, a durability assessment ...

item.title: Openclatura–an Open-Source Nomenclature Framework for Rule-Based Molecule Naming
item.description: Naming molecules systematically remains an open challenge in cheminformatics. The reverse task—converting names to structure—has been largely solved for over a decade by OPSIN, yet no open-source tool performs the forward direction. Here we introduce ...

item.title: Effect of washing on the surface chemistry of silica nanoparticles
item.description: The surface chemistry of silica nanoparticles is central to their use in applications such as catalysis, CO2 adsorption, environmental remediation, or drug delivery, yet the role of post synthesis washing remains poorly understood. The surface of silica ...

item.description: Understanding electrochemical reaction mechanisms requires atomistic simulations that simultaneously capture charged interfaces, explicit solvent, and long-time dynamics, which remain beyond the reach of conventional ab initio molecular dynamics. Here, we ...

item.title: Effects of laser beam irradiation on displacement of microparticles at an air–wet gel interface
item.description: Optical trapping based on radiation pressure has enabled advanced manipulation of microparticles. Previous studies have primarily focused on Gaussian-beam optical trapping of nanoparticles using tightly focused laser beams. In such systems, high-intensity ...

item.title: Transition-State Analysis Reveals a Noncanonical Multi-Step Pathway for O-GlcNAc Transferase Substrate Recognition

item.title: Phonon Spectra Reveal How Selective Fluorination Enhances Hole Transport in Benzodithiophenes (BDT)
item.description: Selective fluorination is one of the most widely used chemical modifications for improving hole mobility in donor-type organic semiconductors. It is commonly assumed that the benefits arise from isolated geometric or electronic descriptors, such as a ...

item.title: Deep-AGAPE: An Open-source Web-Based Machine Learning Tool for Predicting G-Quadruplex Ligand Stabilization
item.description: The identification of biologically active molecules within a vast chemical space remains a major challenge in drug discovery. In this context, G-quadruplexes (G4s), non-canonical nucleic acid structures involved in gene regulation and cancer-related ...

item.title: Metal-Organic Cages to functionalize CNTs: higher dispersibility and phase-transport
item.description: Multi-walled carbon nanotubes (MWCNTs) have potential for a range of industrial applications. However, their utility is constrained by challenges related to processing and dispersibility due to their tendency to aggregate in solvents, especially polar ...

item.title: Coherent Pulsed Dynamic Nuclear Polarization with Endogenous Metal-Ions
item.description: Dynamic nuclear polarization (DNP) can overcome the intrinsically low sensitivity of nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) and enables structural characterization of a wide range of materials. In inorganic solids, DNP can be achieved by introducing ...

item.title: Creatine as an Organocatalyst for the Synthesis of 2,3-Dihydroquinazolin-4(1H)-one Derivatives via Multi-component Reactions at Room Temperature
item.description: Creatine's potential as an organocatalyst for organic synthesis is demonstrated specifically for the preparation of 2,3-dihydroquinazolin-4(1H)-one derivatives. Use of a multi-component sequence involving aldehydes, isatoic anhydride, and ammonium acetate ...

item.title: California’s plan to decarbonize electricity omits key greenhouse gas emissions
item.description: Regional decarbonization or climate action plans in the US generally ignore the greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions of renewable electricity generation systems that occur beyond the operational stage. Our analysis of California’s prospective electricity sector ...

item.title: A Cross-Species Comparison of Excitation Energy Transfer in Red-Lineage Photosystem I
item.description: The Photosystem I supercomplex (PSI) is a central component of photosynthesis in plants and algae. Its antenna subunits absorb the light energy and transfer it through an interconnected pigment network to the reaction center (RC), where it is stored as ...

item.title: The Human ADME Score: A Quantitative Metric of Drug-likeness for Orally Administered Compounds
item.description: Quantitative assessment of ADME-related drug-likeness remains challenging as small-molecule drug discovery expands into larger and more complex chemical space. We hypothesized that drug-likeness for orally administered compounds is best quantified at the ...

item.title: A physical aid to site-selective modification of a peptide: targeting of single residues for conjugation, stapling, and labeling with a nanopore and mechanical positioning
item.description: A physical device to aid post-synthetic modification of specific single residues in a peptide at the single molecule level is proposed; it is similar to a structure that was recently proposed for peptide synthesis without side-chain protection (doi: ...

item.title: NovoExpert-2: State-of-the-Art ADMET Prediction via Gradient-Boosted Trees on MapLight Fingerprints and GIN Embeddings
item.description: We present NovoExpert-2, a dual-method approach to molecular property prediction that achieves state-of-the-art performance on five endpoints of the Therapeutics Data Commons (TDC) ADMET benchmark. Our primary model combines MapLight-style fingerprint fea-...

item.title: A Density Matrix Theory for Photothermal Two-Dimensional Infrared Spectroscopy
item.description: Two-dimensional infrared (2D-IR) spectroscopy can resolve vibrational anharmonicity, mode coupling, and energy transfer. The conventional 2D-IR detects nonlinear polarization that generates propagating photons after sample’s light matter interaction with ...

item.title: ReactiveLJ: A Coordination-Dependent Lennard-Jones-Based Potential for Reversible Bonding in Coarse-Grained Simulations
item.description: Associative polymer networks are cross-linked by reversible bonds whose continual formation, dissociation, and exchange rearrange network topology and thereby govern viscoelastic and transport properties. Coarse-grained (CG) molecular simulations offer a ...

item.title: Photoactivated chlorine dioxide oxygenation of freshly cleaved HOPG surfaces
A Glucopyranosylamine-Based Amorphous Copper Metal-Organic Framework (Cu-aMOF):An Efficient Adsorbent for Chromium ions
item.description: A glucopyranosylamine-derived copper-based amorphous metal-organic framework (Cu-aMOF) was synthesized by the reaction between bis-(4,6-O-ethylidene-β-D-glucopyranosylamine)-1,4- dihydroxy-2,5-dibenzylidene (H6L) and Cu(OAc)2.H2O and evaluated for ...

item.title: Mind the Driver: Behavior-aware Battery Usage Prediction to Reduce Electric Vehicle Range Anxiety
item.description: Range anxiety remains a major concern for electric vehicle (EV) drivers due to unpredictable charge usage influenced by terrain and user behavior variations. To address this issue, we propose a data-driven approach to provide accurate trip-specific ...

item.title: Multiplexed Imaging through Affinity Hierarchies in a Supramolecular Host
item.description: Bioorthogonal chemistry has transformed molecular imaging by enabling highly selective synthetic labeling of biomolecules in their native habitats. However, multiplexed imaging remains fundamentally constrained by the limited number of mutually orthogonal ...

item.title: Lifetime- and potential-engineered photocatalysts for uphill photoredox catalysis
item.description: Most molecular photocatalysts (PCs) have lifetimes ranging from a few nanoseconds to the low microsecond range suitable for photoinduced electron transfer reactions with driving forces larger than –0.2 eV. Very long excited state lifetimes of the PC can ...

item.title: Sb-Doped SrSnO3: a lead free perovskite oxide for sustainable photoresponsive optoelectronics
item.description: Lead free perovskite oxides are emerging as promising alternatives to halide perovskites for stable optoelectronic applications. Building on our previous work demonstrating the potential of 5% Sb-doped SrSnO3 as a transparent conductive oxide, this study ...

item.title: Solution-state Magnetic Bistability in Cobalt Corrole Complexes with Switchable Sidearms
item.description: Molecular materials having switchable electronic states are crucial for modern applications such as spintronics and quantum information processing. In this direction, Ni(II)porphyrins with switchable internal axial ligands have been shown to be the first ...

item.title: In-context learning meets small molecule property prediction: benchmarking novel machine learning approaches
item.description: Recently, a new category of machine learning approaches for tabular data has emerged: tabular foundation models (TFM), based on in-context learning. A TFM is a neural network (usually a transformer) pre-trained primarily on synthetic data. Its input is an ...

item.title: Novel Theoretical Framework for Coupled Transport in Polymer Melt Extrusion
item.description: Coupled transport phenomena in polymer extrusion have been extensively investigated using analytical, constitutive, and numerical models. While these approaches provide detailed descriptions of individual physical mechanisms, a unified theoretical ...

item.title: Quasi-Isostructural Alloying of Cu4TiSe4 with Cu3TMQ4 (TM: V, Nb, Ta; Q: S, Se): Tuning ‘Stuffing’ Concentration for Ultralow Thermal Conductivity
item.description: In this study, we adopted a quasi-isostructural alloying strategy between selenotitanate Cu4TiSe4and sulvanite-type Cu3TMQ4(TM: V, Nb, Ta;Q: S, Se). At room temperature, both systems crystallize in the cubic 𝑃4̅3𝑚 space group, where Cu4TiSe4...

item.title: Cation competition and low pH protect protocell membranes from divalent cation disruption
item.description: Divalent cations such as Mg2+and Ca2+are essential for many modern biochemical processes and likely played similarly important roles during the origin of life. However, these ions are highly disruptive to fatty acid vesicles, creating a fundamental ...

item.title: L→S2+←L Complexes (Sulfurones) with Unique Architecture: Synthesis, Crystal Structure and Quantum Chemical Analysis
item.description: Many innovative complexes with (NHC)→S (NHC is N-Heterocyclic Carbene) coordination bonds were theoretically designed and synthetically obtained in the past five years. Taking this as a clue, (NHC)→S2+←(NHC) systems were proposed as sulfurones. In this ...

item.title: ChemMate: an autonomous research mate for the full computational chemistry research lifecycle, from ideation to manuscript
item.description: Agentic research systems have split along a line. Systems that automate the research lifecycle, from ideation to manuscript, now reach flagship venues, but their experiments are machine-learning runs, biomedical hypotheses, or self-written software, not ...

item.title: Quality over quantity in fluorinated battery interphases
item.description: Fluorination of the solid-electrolyte interphase (SEI) is central to achieving high cycling efficiency in lithium metal batteries. However, current approaches often rely on large amounts of fluorinated salts, solvents, or diluents, increasing electrolyte ...

item.title: Reinforcement of Polyvinyl Chloride Using Plasma-Activated Aramid Fibers and Surface-Bonded Compatibilizer
item.description: This study investigated the application of a two-step compatibilization procedure to aramid fiber–poly(vinyl chloride) (PVC) composites. Aramid fibers were subjected to high radio frequency power air plasma treatment and incorporated into the PVC matrix ...

item.title: Self-centring structural damping with MOF-based compressible liquids
item.description: Introducing hydrophobic cavities to water makes a compressible liquid that can absorb mechanical energy. Herein, we present a self-centring, energy-absorbing damping technology based on this material concept and demonstrate its potential in seismic ...

item.title: The Oil Well as the Mine of the Future: Towards In-situ Coproduction of Critical Minerals and Hydrocarbons
item.description: The global demand for critical minerals and materials (CMM) is rapidly outpacing conventional supply chains. This Perspective presents a paradigm shift by demonstrating that mature U.S. sedimentary basins are not merely depleting fuel reservoirs but are ...

item.title: Saturated Organic Poly-ionomer Films Match Proteins as Surprising Electronic Conductors
item.description: We demonstrate efficient long-range electronic transport through ultrathin (<100 nm) multilayers of highly charged, non-conjugated polyionomers (PIs), a class of organic materials fundamentally distinct from conventional organic semiconductors. Layer-by-...

item.title: A Computational Study of Gemcitabine Encapsulation in CC3 Porous Organic Cage: Molecular Docking, Molecular Dynamics, Binding Free Energy, and Structure-Activity Relationship Analysis
item.description: Gemcitabine, a first-line nucleoside analogue chemotherapy drug, is rapidly inactivated in plasma by cytidine deaminase (CDA), severely limiting its therapeutic efficacy. Porous organic cages (POCs) have emerged as promising drug delivery vehicles due to ...

item.title: Light-Driven Control of Polar Order in Ferroelectric Nematic Liquid Crystals
item.description: Ferroelectric nematic (NF) liquid crystals are polar fluids that combine long-range orientational order with spontaneous macroscopic polarization and exceptionally large dielectric permittivity. While these properties make NF materials attractive for ...

item.title: Integrated GEMC–MD Simulation Study of Phase Behavior and Interfacial Structure in the Benzene–Methanol Azeotrope
item.description: Azeotropes, mixtures that exhibit constant boiling points and behave as single substances, pose significant challenges in separation processes and are crucial in various industrial applications such as chemical manufacturing and purification. In this ...

item.title: Collision Induced Unfolding Predicts the Aggregation of Glycoengineered Monoclonal Antibodies
item.description: Monoclonal antibody (mAb) therapeutics exhibit substantial structural heterogeneity, requiring analytical methods capable of rapidly assessing critical quality attributes (CQAs) that influence stability and developability. Non-native aggregation is a key ...

item.title: MolecuAR: A Multilingual WebAR Molecular Visualization Tool Connected to PubChem for Chemical Education
item.description: Three-dimensional molecular representation is important in chemistry education, yet preparing interactive augmented-reality resources can require specialized authoring and molecule-specific assets. MolecuAR is an open, mobile-first WebAR educational ...

item.title: CatGo: Bridging CLI Coding Agents with Interactive Structure and Workflow Management for Computational Chemistry
item.description: Computational chemistry is increasingly limited not by individual calculations but by the manual effort of assembling, parameterizing, executing, and monitoring multi-step workflows across heterogeneous engines and computing environments; existing ...

item.title: SurfKit: Multi-property surfactant prediction combining experimental data and molecular dynamics simulations
N-Substituent Effects on Selectivity in Ni-Mediated Decarbonylation of N-Arylphthalimides
item.description: The nickel-catalyzed decarbonylation of imides and amides is a promising molecular editing strategy for generating C-C and C-N coupled structures commonly found in biologically active molecules. Despite their potential utility, there is limited ...

item.title: Spin–Phonon Coupling in Molecular Qubits and Organosuperelasticity in Molecular Crystals: A Review
item.description: Molecular symmetry, rooted in group theory, provides a unifying language that connects the spatial arrangement of atoms to the electronic, magnetic, and mechanical behaviour of matter. This review examines two arenas in which symmetry-based reasoning has ...

item.title: Integrating GC-MS with Computational Toxicology for Pesticide Risk Assessment
item.description: Pesticide risk assessment separates analytical detection from toxicological evaluation, leaving a practical gap: regulatory-compliant residue levels reveal nothing about the biological hazard of detected compounds, and computational toxicology models are ...

item.title: Ion Mobility-Selected Collision Induced Unfolding Differentiates Biosimilars Based on Glycan Content
item.description: As patents expire for monoclonal antibody (mAb) therapeutics, biosimilar development has emerged as a critical strategy for improving therapeutic accessibility. One critical quality attribute, glycosylation, can alter the efficacy, stability, and half-...

item.title: Digital Analytical Twins: A Specimen-Centric Architecture for Self-Driving Analytical Laboratories
item.description: Self-driving laboratories increasingly combine robotic handling, automated instruments, workflow orchestration, machine learning, digital twins and autonomous experiment planners. In this literature the central question is usually framed as: which ...

item.title: Ion Mobility-Collision Induced Unfolding Detects Stability Shifts within Hidden Protein Conformer Families
item.description: Proteins commonly exist in complex mixtures, adopting a diverse ensemble of structural conformations. While some of these conformations are functionally important, others (e.g. pre-aggregative states), can adversely affect protein function. In monoclonal ...

item.title: A Unified Total Synthesis of Eudesmane-Type Sesquiterpenoid Dimers Chlorahupetenes A, B and E.
item.description: Chlorahupetenes A (1) and B (2) are eudesmane-type sesquiterpenoid dimers that contain unprecedented, rearranged carbon skeletons that were isolated as enantiomeric pairs by Liu and coworkers in 2022. Herein, we disclose the first total synthesis of ...

item.title: A Synthesis of a Fluorinated Tetrapeptide Substrate, (4-Fluoro-F)LQS, to Monitor SARS-CoV-2 Main Protease Activity (Nonstructural Protein 5, NSP5) Supports Cleavage between Leucine and Glutamine
item.description: Severe acute respiratory coronavirus-2 (SARS-CoV-2) caused the global pandemic in 2020. The inhibition of NSP5 of SARS-CoV-2, the main cysteine protease, is a strategy to kill the virus. This paper describes in more detail the eSorts that were involved in ...

item.title: Compensated ferrimagnetism above 600 K in a metal-organic framework
item.description: Compensated ferrimagnets combine long-range magnetic order with small stray magnetic fields, but realizing such behavior over broad temperature ranges in chemically designable materials remains challenging. Recently, radical-bridged pyrazine frameworks ...

item.title: Capturing Activity Trends in Metal-Organic Framework Catalysis using Minimal Active Site Models
item.description: Metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) offer immense potential as catalysts owing to their modular and tunable structures, yet their vast combinatorial design space makes discovery challenging. Although realistic models of MOF active sites are effective for ...

item.title: High-throughput multireference calculations of magnetic properties of lanthanide complexes
item.description: Molecular magnets based on lanthanides are promising candidates for quantum information technologies. However, progress toward practical applications is limited by the small number of compounds with useful magnetic properties. Computational screening ...

item.title: Operando observation of immobilized carbonic anhydrase for CO2 conversion
item.description: Heterogeneous catalysis with immobilized enzymes is pivotal for continuous and sustainable industrial bioprocesses. However, the relationship between enzyme dispersion on solid supports and catalytic activity is not well described. Here, we investigate ...

item.title: Mechanochemical C‑Terminal Activation of Peptides: Solvent‑Minimal Thiocarboxylation with Suppressed Epimerization
item.description: A solvent‑minimal mechanochemical approach enables one‑step peptide C‑terminal thiocarboxylation with efficient suppression of epimerization. The method affords peptide thiocarboxylic acids from diverse substrates, including epimerization‑prone dipeptides,...

item.title: Nickel-Catalyzed Enantioselective Radical C(sp3)–C(sp3) Cross-Coupling
item.description: Enantioselective radical–radical cross-coupling represents a powerful yet largely unsolved strategy for constructing stereogenic C(sp3) centers, because simultaneously controlling cross-selectivity and absolute stereochemistry between two distinct, ...

item.title: Epimer-Dependent Solid-State Landscapes of Trospium and Exotrospium Halide Salts
item.description: Trospium (TR) and exotrospium (ETR) form a closely related epimeric pair of quaternary ammonium compounds, differing only in the stereochemistry of the tropane ring system. While the solid-state chemistry of trospium has been studied mainly for its ...

item.title: Spatial placement of half-Salen-bound nickel single-atoms on MOFs governs cooperative photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
item.description: The integration of single-atom catalysts into metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) offers a promising strategy for coupling molecular catalytic sites with photoactive architectures. While previous studies have focused primarily on the identity and coordination ...
